principle: Paragraph structure: one theme, first sentence declares it for scanning, last sentence bridges to next
explanation: X.copy|paragraph_structure: one_paragraph == one_theme; first_sentence == declaration_of_topic + keywords_early; last_sentence == conclusion + bridge_to_next; 3-9_lines_optimal; NO:starting_with_"for_example"/"however"/"in_this_case" eg: "And_vice_versa:_if_you..." >> "To_choose_headphones_answer_these_questions:..."

principle: Sentences serve paragraphs not rules — weak syntax may be right if it serves the paragraph's main idea
explanation: X.copy|paragraph_context: sentence_that_looks_weak_alone -> may_be_perfect_in_paragraph; "from_different_viewpoints" vague_alone -> in_paragraph_unfolds_into_ethical + scientific; adding_actors_mechanically -> may_hijack_attention_from_real_topic; NO:blind_syntax_rules_ignoring_paragraph_purpose eg: adding_"animal_rights_activists_advise" -> distracts_from_the_point_about_viewpoints

principle: Structure serves the thought — if one sentence reveals it fully, one-sentence paragraph is enough
explanation: X.copy|dont_pad: paragraph_template == useful_scaffold NOT mandatory_filler; if_one_sentence_says_it_all -> done; three_sentences_repeating_same_idea -> monotone; NO:mechanical_three-part_padding eg: "no_safe_alcohol_dose._Even_a_glass_a_week_impairs_cognition._Simply_put_each_drink_makes_you_dumber._So_even_a_little_is_harmful" >> first_sentence_alone_is_enough

principle: Lists need parallel structure and reader's search scenario — start each element from what the reader is looking for
explanation: X.copy|list_design: elements_must_have_same_structure + same_field_order; build_from_reader_perspective: reader_knows_WHO_they_are -> match_to_tariff; NO:author_taxonomy_first; uneven_element == structural_break -> extract_the_outlier; repeated_info_across_all_elements -> extract_above eg: "child_tariff._For_kids_under_12" >> "kids_under_12_—_child_tariff"

principle: When list elements outgrow the format — use paragraphs with headings; complex data wants a table
explanation: X.copy|list_limits: element_is_a_whole_paragraph? -> make_it_a_paragraph_with_subheading; list == glanceable_homogeneous_items; paragraph == content_that_needs_unpacking; "here_is_what" before_list == crutch: fix_the_intro_sentence_instead; multi-dimensional_data -> pseudo-table; NO:forcing_long_content_into_bullet_format

principle: After editing text may become robotic — vary sentence length, reconnect related thoughts, diversify openings
explanation: X.copy|monotone_fix: all_short_sentences + same_structure == choppy_robotic; fix: merge_related_sentences_back; vary_length_short+long; diversify_openings_with_connectors; words_deleted_as_filler_in_ch1 -> sometimes_restored_for_flow; NO:uniform_sentence_length NO:every_sentence_standalone eg: "dangerous._October._Dark._67%_drivers" >> "October_is_worst_because_dark_early+no_snow._67%_crashes_drivers_fault_but_pedestrians_can_protect"
